verses.text: Og of Bashan comes out; God delivers him to Israel. Og. The second king falls.
Og of Bashan comes out; God delivers him to Israel. Og. The second king falls.
Og of Bashan comes out; God delivers him to Israel. Og. The second king falls.
They take all his sixty cities and the spoil. Cities. The land is wholly given.
They take all his sixty cities and the spoil. Cities. The land is wholly given.
They take all his sixty cities and the spoil. Cities. The land is wholly given.
They take all his sixty cities and the spoil. Cities. The land is wholly given.
Two kings east of Jordan are subdued; Og's great bedstead is noted. Subdued. God gives the eastern land.
Two kings east of Jordan are subdued; Og's great bedstead is noted. Subdued. God gives the eastern land.
Two kings east of Jordan are subdued; Og's great bedstead is noted. Subdued. God gives the eastern land.
Two kings east of Jordan are subdued; Og's great bedstead is noted. Subdued. God gives the eastern land.
The land is given to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh. Divided. The east tribes receive their portion.
The land is given to Reuben, Gad, and half Manasseh. Divided. The east tribes receive their portion.
Their borders are described. Bounds. Each has his place.
Their borders are described. Bounds. Each has his place.
Their borders are described. Bounds. Each has his place.
Their borders are described. Bounds. Each has his place.
They must go armed before their brethren till all have rest. The pledge. They will help win the rest.
They must go armed before their brethren till all have rest. The pledge. They will help win the rest.
They must go armed before their brethren till all have rest. The pledge. They will help win the rest.
Moses charges Joshua, 'fear them not: for the LORD... shall fight for you.' Courage. God fights for His people.
Moses charges Joshua, 'fear them not: for the LORD... shall fight for you.' Courage. God fights for His people.
Moses begs to cross over and see the good land. The plea. He longs to enter.
Moses begs to cross over and see the good land. The plea. He longs to enter.
Moses begs to cross over and see the good land. The plea. He longs to enter.
God refuses but bids him view it from Pisgah and charge Joshua. Refused. He sees but enters not.
God refuses but bids him view it from Pisgah and charge Joshua. Refused. He sees but enters not.
